items.title: It’s not *quite* like Jay Cutler breaking Sid Luckman’s 65-year-old touchdown record for the Chicago Bears…but Salvador Perez being on the verge of breaking George Brett’s home run record for the Royals is something - especially in modern baseball.
items.author: Jim Owczarski
items.body_excerpt:
It’s not *quite* like Jay Cutler breaking Sid Luckman’s 65-year-old touchdown record for the Chicago Bears…but Salvador Perez being on the verge of breaking George Brett’s home run record for the Royals is something - especially in modern baseball.

Brett retired in 1993.

items.title: Give it time. Seattle about to start to do what Washington is doing—taking down these top teams.
items.author: Zena Keita
items.body_excerpt:
Give it time. Seattle about to start to do what Washington is doing—taking down these top teams. 

Washington now has wins over Vegas, Golden State, and Minnesota.

Seattle’s play against Indiana and Minnesota recently has shown me a lot…I’m keeping my eye on them the second half of the season.

items.title: Summer League is chaotic. Somewhere in that chaos are numbers that tell the story of what these rookies will actually look like in the regular season.
items.author: Hoop Informatics
items.body_excerpt:
Summer League is chaotic. Somewhere in that chaos are numbers that tell the story of what these rookies will actually look like in the regular season.

In this piece, you'll learn which Summer League stats are meaningful & which ones you should mostly ignore, with data for every rookie who played.

media.title: Summer League Stats You Can Trust: 2026 Edition

items.title: Olivia Miles is the fastest player in WNBA history to reach 500 PTS and 150 AST, doing it in just 26 games 🤯
items.author: Mark Jones
items.body_excerpt:
Olivia Miles is the fastest player in WNBA history to reach 500 PTS and 150 AST, doing it in just 26 games 🤯

She breaks the previous marks set by Paige Bueckers (28 games) and Caitlin Clark (29 games).

items.title: After three seasons on the Blazers TV broadcast, I was informed that my contract would not be renewed.
items.author: Tom Haberstroh
items.body_excerpt:
After three seasons on the Blazers TV broadcast, I was informed that my contract would not be renewed. 

I am grateful for being a part of the best broadcast crew in the NBA and capturing the rise of the team. 

I wish nothing but the best for the terrific staff and Rip City fans. ✌️

items.title: Fever (17-10) go 3-1 on homestand, winning 8 of 11 entering break. 123-88 over Sun
items.author: Scott Agness
items.body_excerpt:
Fever (17-10) go 3-1 on homestand, winning 8 of 11 entering break. 123-88 over Sun

- Record 70pts in a half
- Record 123pts
- Led by as many as 35
- Clark with 27/11a, 16th time with at least 20/10
- Mitchell (23pts) with 20+ pts and 3+ 3s in 11 straight games.

Next: All-Star Weekend

items.title: Fever score the seventh-most points by any team ever and blast Connecticut 123-88. Highest field goal percentage by the Fever in a single game (61.6%). Their three stars combined for 72 points. A dominant outing for win number 17. Fever enter the break 17-10.
items.author: Tony East
items.body_excerpt: Fever score the seventh-most points by any team ever and blast Connecticut 123-88. Highest field goal percentage by the Fever in a single game (61.6%). Their three stars combined for 72 points. A dominant outing for win number 17. Fever enter the break 17-10.

media.title: Phoenix Suns Offseason All-Access Episode 1: Mr. Arizona
media.description: Episode one of Phoenix Suns Offseason All-Access, presented by Mortgage Matchup, showcases the war room as the Suns made moves to aquire rights to the 30th pick in the 2026 NBA Draft. Moving in position to bring Koa Peat, aka Mr. Arizona, to his hometown professional team.  Then follow Koa's first few days in Phoenix as he meets the team, conducts his first press conference and throws out a first pitch at the Arizona Diamondbacks game.  https://mortgagematchup.com/  #SunsUp  Subscribe to get all the latest Phoenix Suns Content: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCLxlWVVHz2a8SdCfxzVXzQw  Connect with the Phoenix Suns online and on Social:  🔵  Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/suns/  🔵  Twitter: https://twitter.com/Suns  🟣  Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/suns/  💻  Website: https://www.nba.com/suns/?tmd=1  🟢  TikTok: @phoenixsuns  🟡  SnapChat: phoenix suns  🎟️  Join us for a Game: Suns.com/Tickets
